methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Stratolaunch is a private-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Paul Allen and Burt Rutan, currently owned by Cerberus. The valuation landscape comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Blue Origin, SpaceX, Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs—a total of 13 comparable entities across the sector.

Two standard triangulation methods are inapplicable here. Peer-median analysis cannot proceed: while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints, making sector-wide aggregation unreliable and potentially misleading. Public-comp anchoring, though applicable in principle, is blocked by the absence of Stratolaunch's revenue on file—a standard limitation for private companies. The Space & Aerospace public median trades at 49.8x price-to-sales across the 6-ticker universe, but without disclosed revenue, this multiple cannot be applied.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without either revenue disclosure from Stratolaunch or access to deeper private financing data on the peer set. The key uncertainty is whether transaction-level data on recent fundraises by SpaceX, Blue Origin, or other Stage-peer companies exists; if available, those would provide the most direct anchoring. Until such inputs materialize, valuation hinges on deal-specific due diligence rather than quantitative market comparables.
